value:
SBI Card, a subsidiary of the State Bank of India, stands as the second-largest credit card issuer in India. With a customer base of 2.08 Crore cardholders, we provide financial accessibility through an extensive range of value-added payment products and services, catering to both transactional and short-term credit needs. Our commitment to delivering a diverse credit card portfolio enables seamless and secure cashless, contactless digital payments in India. A key driver of our success lies in cultivating and refining a varied product mix. SBI Card serves a broad spectrum of cardholders, spanning from the "super-premium" and "premium" categories to the "affluent," "mass affluent," "mass," and "new to credit" segments.

value:
At SBI Card, we embrace Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) principles not as peripheral commitments, but as central to building a resilient, responsible, and future-ready organization. In the past year, we took deliberate steps to strengthen our ESG foundation, including a refreshed materiality assessment that sharpened our strategic priorities and guided our actions across all dimensions of sustainability.

Across all dimensions of ESG, we made purposeful progress. We intensified our efforts to cut emissions, become more energy efficient, and align our operations with a low-carbon future. We also made a conscious shift toward more sustainable practices — from reducing physical materials to strengthening initiatives that empower and support our people and communities.

On the social side, we remain committed to developing a workplace that is more diverse, more inclusive, and more adaptable. We invested in strengthening skills, capabilities, and awareness across all levels of the organization, and we deepened our engagement with the communities we serve — extending education, health care, and other initiatives to help drive meaningful change.

Governance remained a key priority in our sustainability journey. We maintained robust controls, enhanced oversight mechanisms, and integrated consideration of ESG risks into our enterprise risk framework — reflecting our ongoing resolve to operate with fairness, integrity, and accountability.

As we move forward, we will continue to pursue a path of responsible growth — strengthening our ability to innovate, adapt, and contribute positively to society. Our material priorities will guide us, and our progress will be driven by collaboration, discipline, and a deep sense of responsibility toward all our stakeholders.

Thank You!

Ms. Salila Pande
Managing Director & Chief Executive Officer

Yes, our publicly available ‘Code of Conduct’ includes guidelines on anti-corruption and anti-bribery. This policy is applicable to everyone associated with the company – from our employees and suppliers to contractors, third-party representatives, and consultants. It reinforces our commitment to uphold ethical practices across all facets of our business operations. The key elements of these guidelines include, and not limited to:
• A ban on bribes in any form (including kickbacks) on any portion of contract payments or soft dollar practices
• The implementation of adequate internal controls designed to prevent and identify any acts of bribery; and
• The upkeep of precise and detailed accounting records that accurately reflect the company's financial transactions.
• Reflagging cases for any direct and indirect political contributions.

SBI Card is a non-manufacturing, service-based financial institution. The primary source of plastic usage in SBI Card’s operations is plastic credit cards issued to customers. At the end of the card’s lifecycle (expired or blocked cards), customers are encouraged to cut and dispose of cards securely.
SBI Card has already initiated the adoption of rPVC (recycled PVC) cards, with a gradual rollout strategy to increase its share across the portfolio in the coming years.

Yes, SBI Card is dedicated to maintaining a safe and supportive workplace. This is facilitated through a reliable and well-established grievance resolution procedure for employees. Permanent employees can utilize an anonymous 24/7 online portal to express concerns, grievances, or provide suggestions. Employees also have the option to submit complaints via email to the Ombuds.

The procedure for handling employee complaint emails is as follows:

i.	An inquiry is initiated upon initial evaluation of the complaint.
ii.	Investigators are assigned to examine the case.
iii.	The complainant's identity is kept confidential and protected throughout the inquiry.
iv.	The inquiry report is submitted for decision-making, adhering to the Corrective Action Policy

value:
Employee Health Initiatives
SBI Card prioritizes the health of the employees through a range of measures:
1. Maintaining a safe and hygienic workplace with daily cleaning and sanitization.
2. Ensuring better air quality in office premises via the periodic cleaning and sanitization of AC air filters.
3. Providing masks for employees and visitors as needed to prevent the spread of contagious diseases.
4. Offering onsite medical facilities at SBIC offices where the employee strength exceeds 500.
5. Improving indoor air quality by deploying oxygen-generating indoor plants.
6. Implementing a wellness program to support the holistic well-being of employees.
7. Organizing multiple wellness sessions, including yoga, meditation, and Zumba, within the office.
8. Facilitating preventive annual health check-ups for employees.
9. Providing a Mediclaim facility for employees and their dependents.
10. Offering an in-house, state-of-the-art gymnasium at the Gurgaon office.
11. Regularly communicating through various channels to enhance awareness of health and hygiene.

Employee Safety Measures
SBI Card is committed to ensuring the safety of the employees through the following provisions:
1. Guarding all offices to ensure employee safety.
2. Implementing physical access control systems in all offices to restrict access to authorized personnel only within SBIC premises.
3. Maintaining 24x7 CCTV surveillance to monitor for any suspicious activity.
4. Ensuring all offices comply with dual exits for safe evacuations during emergencies.
5. Placing a sufficient number of fire extinguishers in all offices.
6. Installing fire detection and control systems.
7. Equipping all SBIC offices with a Public Address (PA) system.

We conduct self-audits on a quarterly basis across all SBI Card offices, where the implementation of EHS parameters is thoroughly checked by competent and experienced professionals from our Administration function. The results of these self audits are carefully reviewed and serve as the basis for any necessary improvements in our office environments. This effective approach optimises health and safety conditions for our employees, demonstrating our strong commitment to their welfare.

To enhance stakeholder engagement, we implemented a robust mechanism guided by the principles of inclusion, materiality, and responsiveness for identifying and prioritizing stakeholders. We classified stakeholder groups based on their significance to our business and their roles in both upstream and downstream processes, as well as their relationships within internal and external environments. The following steps were taken to identify relevant stakeholder groups:
1. Internal Brainstorming: We engaged management at all levels to establish criteria for stakeholders by addressing key questions, such as:
• Who are the individuals, groups, or institutions interested in our core operations?
• Who stands to benefit from our core operations and activities?
• Who may be adversely affected by our core operations?
• Who possesses the power to influence our organizational activities, particularly our core operations?
2. Identification of Stakeholders: Using the established criteria, we identified individuals and entities (both internal and external) that influence or are impacted by our core operations and activities.
3. Determining Engagement Modes: For each identified group, we defined the mode of engagement—such as discussions, written communication, stakeholder meetings, or sustainability reports—and established the frequency of these interactions.
4. Segregation of Stakeholders: We categorized stakeholders based on their level of impact, frequency of interactions, communication methods, interest in core operations, and influence on our activities.
5. Mapping and Consensus: We mapped the stakeholder groups along with their modes and frequencies of engagement, obtaining consensus from senior management on the identified groups.
6. Development of a Stakeholder Engagement Plan: We aim to refine this process into a comprehensive 'stakeholder engagement plan' that will serve as a blueprint for future communications and engagement strategies.
This structured approach ensures that we effectively engage with our stakeholders, fostering meaningful relationships and enhancing our organizational responsiveness.

value:
As part of its commitment to sustainability, SBI Card has implemented a series of energy-efficient initiatives aimed at reducing its carbon footprint and enhancing operational efficiency. A major milestone was achieved at the Chennai office, where a transition to renewable energy took place in November 2024. In collaboration with the building owner and a green energy provider, solar power is now being sourced. This shift has enabled approximately 60–80% of the Chennai office’s electricity consumption to be met through renewable sources. As a result, the company recorded a reduction of around 1,05,000 kilograms of CO2 emissions compared to the previous year. This initiative underscores SBI Card’s focus on adopting clean energy solutions as a key pillar of its ESG strategy.

In addition to the Chennai renewable energy project, SBI Card has introduced several energy optimization measures across its offices. At the Gurgaon headquarters, energy-efficient microwave units were installed in the cafeteria to lower electricity usage. Further, in critical operational areas such as the hub rooms, the number of Precision Air Conditioning (PAC) units in use was reduced from two to one without compromising performance. PAC runtime cycles were also extended from 4 to 8 hours to reduce short cycling, thereby minimizing energy spikes and enhancing system longevity.

Another significant measure involved optimizing the operational hours of Air Handling Units (AHUs) based on actual occupancy. Their daily runtime was reduced from 11–12 hours to 8–9 hours, ensuring energy is not wasted during non-peak periods. Moreover, the Facilities and CSR–ESG teams jointly initiated the “Lights Out for a Greener Tomorrow” campaign to promote awareness and behavioral change toward energy conservation across all offices.

Collectively, these initiatives led to energy savings of approximately 1,30,000 kWh and a further reduction of around 1,06,600 kilograms in Scope 2 CO2 emissions. These actions reflect SBI Card’s broader commitment to sustainability through continuous improvement in energy performance, reinforcing its role as an environmentally responsible organization.

The company is committed to responsible waste disposal across all its locations:
• The plastic waste is disposed through building management team of the building owner. 
• E- waste is disposed through authorised vendors who have been certified in handling E-waste. The vendor segregates (IT/Mobile/CD/CE/PCB), dismantles, separates ferrous, non-ferrous, glass & plastic. It is followed by extraction of base and higher base metals and refinement of metals into 99.9% purity.
• Hazardous waste is not generated through any processes.
• At the Gurgaon office, waste management is overseen by the building authority and handed over to a Municipal Corporation-certified vendor, from whom disposal and recycling certificates are obtained. 

Additionally, SBI Card offices in NSP Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, and Chennai have partnered with authorized service providers to ensure end-to-end waste collection, segregation, and recycling. Remaining SBI Card offices coordinate waste disposal in compliance with local municipal guidelines.

SBI Card has a comprehensive Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery framework that provides guidance to minimise the service disruption impact to the company and its stakeholders including employees, customers in the event of disaster or business disruption. Through detailed business impact analyses, we identify vulnerabilities and develop both short-term and long-term strategies to maintain business operations. 
Simultaneously, our disaster management plan emphasizes the recovery and restoration of data and IT infrastructure, enabling a swift return to full functionality following any disruptive event. This proactive approach ensures that we are well-prepared to handle unforeseen challenges while safeguarding the interests of all stakeholders.

value:
SBI Card does not generate hazardous waste in any of its processes. All waste produced is measured and managed through the building management team.

• E-Waste Disposal: E-waste is handled through authorized vendors certified for proper e-waste disposal.

• Recyclable Waste Management: Other recyclable materials, such as paper and plastic, are also disposed of through the landlord, who implements a separate waste treatment process tailored to the specific type of waste.

This systematic approach ensures responsible waste management and supports our commitment to sustainability.

1. Calling our helpline

3. Webform (Website & Mobile App)
4. Social media
5. Walking Desk Locations
6. Escalation channels (Nodal officer, Principle Nodal officer & CS head)
7. Reaching out to Banking Ombudsman appointed by RBI

Grievance Redressal Policy is available at https://www.sbicard.com/en/grievance-redressal-policy.page

Our Information Security and Cyber Security Policies are aligned with regulatory requirements. We have implemented multiple detective, preventive, and corrective measures to protect our organization’s information assets in line with the ‘Defence in Depth’ strategy. We log and report cyber security incidents and conduct frequent audits and assessments to validate our adherence to various regulatory compliance requirements. The Company has strengthened its data loss prevention systems and deployed various controls to ensure the information/ data of customers, stakeholders, and employees are secure. Information Security Committee regularly reviews the performance of key information and cyber security metrics and provides directions to mitigate risks. We are committed to Information Security and are proud to state that SBI Card is accredited with PCI-DSS 4.0 and ISO 27001:2022 standards. 
Further, the Company remains steadfast in our commitment to upholding the highest standards of privacy and data protection across all our operations. In line with this commitment, we have embarked on a structured journey to achieve compliance with the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA). As disclosed in our recently updated data privacy policy, SBI Card is committed to protecting our customer’s personal information adhering to our key data handling principles of Transparency and Fairness, Purpose Limitation, Data Minimization, Storage Limitation, Data Security, Data Accuracy and Accountability. We also organize regular training programs, awareness sessions for our employees to keep them updated about company objectives towards data privacy and security.

As a credit card issuer, our product details are not displayed on physical labels but are accessible through various touchpoints, including our website, application forms, and welcome kits. 

Compliance with Regulatory Guidelines
• We strictly adhere to the Reserve Bank of India's guidelines, ensuring customers receive comprehensive and relevant information about our credit cards at all stages and interaction points.

Transparent Customer Interactions
• We prioritize clear and open communication. Our sales representatives provide prospects with the "Most Important Terms and Conditions" (MITC) along with other critical product details.
• Following the Fair Practice Code, we share in-depth information about various credit card types and features, empowering customers to make well-informed decisions.

Customer Consent and Onboarding
• Customers provide explicit consent to our terms and conditions, and we reiterate key details during the onboarding process to ensure clarity.

Customer Awareness and Engagement
• We actively foster awareness through emails, SMS alerts, and digital platforms such as our website, mobile app, and social media channels like Facebook.
• We regularly assess the customer experience through our internal Transactional CSAT process, allowing us to refine and improve services based on valuable customer feedback.
